rules.summary: Always rebuild dist/ from src/ before claiming runtime parity; generated runtime can lag even when core checks pass.
Verify generated CLI behavior with direct commands after npm run build; do not rely only on build exit code.
dist/ is .gitignored; do not use git diff --stat dist for review evidence.
npm run check:tokens may report pre-existing adapter-block warnings for agents not installed; classify as non-blocking unless that adapter is installed.
Package checks (npm run check, npm run check:tokens, npm pack --dry-run) must pass or have documented pre-existing warnings before a sprint closes.
Keep release docs and checklists in sync with actual scripts when package.json, CI, or validation commands change.
Run npm run check:links after any documentation change that adds or moves links.
Reference real commands in documentation, not prose approximations; copy exact package.json script names into checklists.
Close a sprint by setting state.json activeSprint to null and nextAction to plan_sprint; never jump to the next sprint before it is generated.
Refresh all summaries (state.json, index.json, sprint summary, roadmap summary, debt summary, re-entry prompts) at sprint close.
Update index.json nextTask to be a verbatim substring of the active sprint Markdown; artifact doctor validates this consistency.
When closing the final sprint in a roadmap, set scope status to completed and nextAction to wrap_up; mark roadmap summary and re-entry prompts as completed.
Prefer kyro context-pack over manual file selection at session start, after compaction, or when resuming a scope.
Pass --json explicitly in fixture checkers that validate context-pack output.
Do not run kyro repair after sprint close; it can corrupt state.json and summaries.
Budget manifest entries must stay provider-neutral; enforce with npm run check:budget-manifest.
Bare --task fallback requires index.nextTask in T{n}.{m} form; prose nextTask needs explicit --task <id>.
